text.title: Prometheus: Application Instrumentation Patterns
text.summary: RED method instrumentation in Python, Node.js, and Go with concrete metric definitions and middleware patterns.

section.title: RED Method
section.p: Instrument every HTTP service with three metrics (RED method): Rate (requests per second), Errors (failed requests per second), Duration (request latency histogram). These three metrics answer all operational questions about a service.

example.note: FastAPI instrumentation: Counter for request count, Histogram for latency, Gauge for in-progress, /metrics endpoint.
example.code:
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, Gauge, generate_latest, CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST from fastapi import FastAPI, Response import time
REQUEST_COUNT = Counter( 'http_requests_total', 'Total HTTP requests', ['method', 'endpoint', 'status'] ) REQUEST_LATENCY = Histogram( 'http_request_duration_seconds', 'HTTP request latency', ['method', 'endpoint'], buckets=[.005, .01, .025, .05, .1, .25, .5, 1, 2.5, 5, 10] ) IN_PROGRESS = Gauge('http_requests_in_progress', 'Requests in progress', ['method', 'endpoint'])
app = FastAPI()
@app.get("/metrics") def metrics(): return Response(content=generate_latest(), media_type=CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST)

example.note: Express middleware for automatic request tracking with prom-client.
Go HTTP middleware using promauto for automatic registration.
